input_context.decision_surface: XRPL validator amendment decision surface for Clawback.
input_context.default_vote_question: Infer the latest stable release source default vote from the source-backed amendment description only.

source_facts.claim: Clawback: For regulatory purposes, some issuers must have the ability to recover issued tokens after they are distributed to accounts. For example, if an issuer were to discover that tokens were sent to an account sanctioned for illegal activity, the issuer could recover, or claw back the funds. Clawback is disabled by default. To use clawback, you must set the lsfAllowTrustLineClawback flag using an AccountSet transaction. See Clawback for details on this amendment.
XRPL amendment voting requires sustained validator supermajority before activation; incompatible servers can become amendment-blocked.
Clawback appears in XRPSCAN with introduced rippled version 1.12.0.
